meta.problem: Every Monday I spend ~3 hours pulling competitor pricing and feature changes into a digest for the team.

agent.name: competitor-digest
agent.model_rationale: Inherits the model you're already signed into Claude Code with — no API key, no per-run cost. Pin 'opus' or 'sonnet' here if you want this agent to ignore the session model.
agent.system_summary: Researches the 5 named competitors' public sites, pricing pages, and changelogs; writes a weekly digest to outputs/digest.md.
never_dos: Never contact anyone or post anywhere — research and drafting only
Never present guesses as facts; mark anything unverified

outcome.description: Produce this week's competitor digest as outputs/digest.md
rubric_criteria: digest.md exists and covers all 5 competitors listed in the task
Every pricing or feature change has a source link
Each competitor section ends with a one-line 'so what for us'
Anything unchanged since last week is listed in one 'no movement' line, not padded out
No claim appears without a source or an explicit 'unverified' tag

tools.permission_notes: The subagent's tools: line names these built-ins; settings.json gates which Bash/WebFetch calls the agent may run without asking.

connections.notes: v0 uses public web only — no local MCP, no .env. Next: add the Notion MCP server to settings.json + a token in .env to read the internal strategy page.

stores.purpose: Last week's snapshot per competitor, so runs report changes rather than re-describing everything.

environment.networking: settings.json allows WebFetch on the competitor domains; everything else asks.

schedule.mechanism: launchd plist (macOS) running `claude -p` headless; a crontab line is included as the cross-platform option. Claude Code's own /schedule is the native alternative.

cases.name: week-of-may-25
cases.expected: The digest Lamis wrote by hand that week

evals.notes: Case 1 is the v0 input; the other two are held back as a regression check. evals/run-evals.sh runs them locally before promoting any new agent version.

interface.how: Digest lands in outputs/; Lamis pastes it into Slack for now. runs/ holds the history.

next_directions.what: Let the agent post the digest to Slack itself
next_directions.why_deferred: Write-action to an external system — kept human-in-the-loop for v0
next_directions.how: Add a Slack MCP server to settings.json + a token in .env; keep it gated so Lamis confirms each post

next_directions.what: Pull the internal strategy doc into context
next_directions.why_deferred: Needs a Notion credential that wasn't on hand during the session
next_directions.how: Add the Notion MCP server locally + a token in .env; reference the doc in the task

next_directions.what: Lock networking down to only the competitor sites
next_directions.why_deferred: Hardening — leaving the rest on 'ask' is fine while the agent is read-only
next_directions.how: Tighten settings.json permissions to allow WebFetch only on the competitor domains; deny the rest

next_directions.what: Re-run the 3 eval cases before promoting any new agent version
next_directions.why_deferred: Process habit, not a build task
next_directions.how: Run evals/run-evals.sh against the new agent file; promote to the schedule only when verdicts hold
